items.front: How long must pass after ETA, clearance void time, or failure to cancel a flight plan before an aircraft is considered overdue under 10-3-1?
items.back: 30 minutes, during which neither communications nor radar contact can be established.

items.front: What three events start the 30-minute overdue clock under 10-3-1?
items.back: 1. ETA over a compulsory reporting point or clearance limit. 2. Clearance void time. 3. VFR/IFR aircraft arriving at a non-towered/non-FSS airport fails to cancel a flight plan.

items.front: When should ALNOT procedures be initiated immediately (without waiting 30 minutes) under 10-3-1?
items.back: When there is an abnormal simultaneous loss of radar and communications with an IFR or VFR/SVFR aircraft receiving flight following. Also when there is reason to believe an aircraft is overdue prior to 30 minutes.

items.front: Which facility forwards overdue aircraft information to the ARTCC under 10-3-2?
items.back: TERMINAL facilities. They must alert the ARTCC when an aircraft is in emergency status that may require SAR, or an IFR aircraft is overdue.

items.front: Which facility alerts the RCC for overdue aircraft under 10-3-3?
items.back: EN ROUTE (ARTCC). When an aircraft is in emergency status or an IFR aircraft is overdue, the ARTCC must alert the RCC.

items.front: What two extra items does the 10-3-3 RCC notification list require that 10-3-2 ARTCC notification list does not?
items.back: Action taken by reporting facility and proposed action (f), and facility and person calling (a). The RCC list has 13 items; the ARTCC list has 11.

items.front: What is the geographic scope of an ALNOT under 10-3-4?
items.back: All centers and Area B circuits, generally 50 miles on either side of the route of flight from the last reported position to destination. At RCC recommendation or controller discretion, may cover the maximum range of the aircraft.

items.front: When must a facility respond to an INREQ or ALNOT under 10-3-4b?
items.back: Within one hour of receipt. Check position records, notify the originator of results, retain the alert in active status, and immediately notify of subsequent contact until cancellation is received.

items.front: What are the three conditions for transferring search responsibility to the RCC under 10-3-5?
items.back: 1. 30 minutes elapsed after estimated fuel exhaustion time. 2. Aircraft not located within one hour after ALNOT issuance. 3. ALNOT search completed with negative results.

items.front: What must be provided to the RCC and SAR Teams regarding the last known position under 10-3-6?
items.back: The most accurate latitude and longitude available using en route and terminal radar sensor data. If that would cause undue delay, use any available method for initial coordinates and follow up with formal data.

items.front: When is an ALNOT cancelled under 10-3-7?
items.back: When directed by the RCC, either when the aircraft is located or the search is abandoned. Include the location where the aircraft or wreckage was found in the cancellation.
